title: ⭐ Recycling Project Officer (Part-time, 3-day/week)
description:
Gratitude Corner is part of the community engagement services, featuring recycling and thrift shop – Gratitude Shop (修福轩).  We are seeking a committed team member to support the Gratitude Corner’s day-to-day operations in the areas of inventory management, pricing, sales, and customer/volunteer engagement etc..

👩🏻‍💼👨🏻‍💼Your Role:

  • Assist in overseeing the overall operations of the Gratitude Corner
  • Support the day-to-day operations in the areas of inventory management, pricing, sales and customer engagement etc.
  • Maintain up-to-date and accurate sales records, activities and other related information
  • Assist in managing and coordinating volunteers
  • Ensure workplace safety and environment cleanliness

📚 What You Bring:

  • Qualification: Min. GCE ‘O’ / ’N’ Level
  • Year(s) of Experience: At least 1 year of work experience. Experience in recycling operations, or thrift/retail management is an advantage
  • Language(s): Bilingual in English and Mandarin to liaise with English and Mandarin speaking stakeholders
  • Required Knowledge/Skill(s): Basic computer skills, Good customer service skills, Effective communication and interpersonal skills, Good organizational and time-management skills
  • Good team player with high level of integrity
  • Physically fit to perform hands-on recycling work, including sorting and lifting goods
  • Able to work on weekend(s), public holiday(s) and irregular working hour(s)

🎊 Your Rewards:

  • Attractive salary commensurate with work experience
  • Delicious vegetarian meals provided to keep you energised throughout the day.
  • Few minutes of walking distance from Bright Hill MRT Station (TE7)
  • And more surprises — join us to discover the full package

postedCompany.description:
Kong Meng San Phor Kark See Monastery (KMSPKS) is a spiritual sanctuary in urban Singapore where one can learn the Buddha’s teachings of wisdom and compassion; practise mindfulness; develop gratitude and bring happiness to all.


Founded in 1921, KMSPKS is one of the first traditional Chinese forest monasteries in Singapore. Today, the monastery sits on 75,470 square metres – equivalent to the size of almost 11 football fields – and is one of the most significant and renowned monasteries in Southeast Asia. Over the years, we have grown dynamically in Dharma propagation, community involvement and religious harmony efforts. Today, we are a community of Buddhists who come together to learn, practise and share the teachings of the Buddha.


Are you looking to use your skills and experience to serve the community for a greater cause?

Apart from a competitive remuneration package and attractive benefits, our culture encourages work-life balance and spiritual development. This is also a place where you will find a great sense of job satisfaction as you see your work impacts the lives of others.


We are looking for dynamic, committed and passionate individuals to drive and fulfill our vision of being an international Buddhist Hub.


Be part of our big family! Let us together, guided by wisdom and compassion, reach out and impact the community.
